overview.title: Real-Time Collaboration
overview.description: Work together with team members on the same 3D model in real time. See cursors, selections, and edits instantly.

create_session.title: Create Collaboration Session
create_session.description: Start a new collaborative design session. Share the room ID with team members to invite them.
create_session.usage: Click Tools → Collaborate → Create Session, enter a name, and share the generated room ID.

join_session.title: Join Collaboration Session
join_session.description: Join an existing collaborative session using the room ID provided by the host.
join_session.usage: Click Tools → Collaborate → Join Session, enter the room ID and your name.

share_room.title: Share Room Link
share_room.description: Generate a shareable link to invite others to your collaboration session. Can optionally password-protect the room.
share_room.usage: In collaboration panel, click 'Share' to copy the link or set a password.
tips: Password-protected rooms are more secure for sensitive projects
Share links expire after 24 hours for security

room_security.title: Room Security & Passwords
room_security.description: Protect your collaboration room with a password. Only users with the correct password can join.
room_security.usage: When creating a room, check 'Require Password' and enter a secure password.
tips: Use strong passwords (min 8 characters, mixed case, numbers)
Change password if room is compromised
Public rooms have no password

user_list.title: Collaboration User List
user_list.description: See all users in the current session with their status, color, and active selections.
user_list.usage: The user list appears in the right panel during a collaboration session. Green dot = online, yellow = idle, red = offline.
tips: Hover over a user to highlight their selection
Right-click to kick user or change permissions

cursor_sharing.title: Cursor Sharing
cursor_sharing.description: See where each team member is looking with live cursor positions. Cursor updates are throttled (10x/sec) for performance.
cursor_sharing.usage: Move your mouse in the 3D viewport. Other users will see a cursor indicator with your name and color.
tips: Cursor color is unique per user for easy identification
Turn off in Settings for privacy

selection_sharing.title: Selection Sharing
selection_sharing.description: When you select a part, all team members see it highlighted in your color. Know what others are working on.
selection_sharing.usage: Click on a part in the 3D view. Other users see the selection with your user color.
tips: Multi-select with Ctrl+Click
Selection updates are instant (no throttling)
Helps avoid conflicting edits

live_chat.title: Collaboration Chat
live_chat.description: Send instant messages to your team without leaving the app. Chat history is saved for the session.
live_chat.usage: Type in the chat box at the bottom right and press Enter to send.
tips: Messages are shown with timestamp and user color
Chat history is saved for 50 most recent messages

mentions.title: Mentions in Chat
mentions.description: Mention a specific team member using @ symbol to get their attention.
mentions.usage: Type @username in chat. Mentioned user gets a notification even if minimized.
tips: @everyone mentions all users in room
Mentions show in bold red text

comments.title: Part Comments
comments.description: Add comments to specific parts for design reviews and feedback.
comments.usage: Right-click on a part → Add Comment. Comments are visible to all team members.
tips: Comments are pinned to parts and appear when part is selected
Resolve comments when discussion is done

design_review.title: Collaborative Design Review
design_review.description: Review a design together in real time. All changes are tracked and can be undone.
design_review.usage: Enable 'Design Review Mode' in Tools → Collaborate. Changes are logged for review.
tips: All changes are reversible
Review history shows who made each change and when
Approve or reject changes from other users

operation_sync.title: Operation Synchronization
operation_sync.description: All geometry operations (extrude, fillet, chamfer, etc.) are synchronized in real time across all clients.
operation_sync.usage: Perform an operation (e.g., extrude). Other users see it applied immediately to their view.
tips: Operations are logged in order for consistency
Offline operations queue and sync when reconnected

conflict_resolution.title: Conflict Resolution
conflict_resolution.description: When two users edit the same part simultaneously, conflicts are resolved using Last-Writer-Wins (LWW) or CRDT algorithm.
conflict_resolution.usage: If conflict occurs, app will notify you. Accept or reject the conflicting change.
tips: Geometry operations use CRDT (order-preserved)
Property changes use LWW (latest timestamp wins)
Use chat to discuss conflicting changes

offline_mode.title: Offline Collaboration
offline_mode.description: Work offline and sync changes when reconnected. Operations queue automatically.
offline_mode.usage: Keep using the app normally when offline. Connection will restore and sync automatically.
tips: Offline queue can hold up to 1000 operations
Sync happens automatically on reconnect
No data is lost

connection_status.title: Connection Status
connection_status.description: Monitor your collaboration server connection. Status bar shows connection state.
connection_status.usage: Look at top-right corner: 🟢 Connected, 🟡 Reconnecting, 🔴 Disconnected.
tips: 'Reconnecting' state lasts up to 30 seconds before showing disconnected
All changes made offline will sync when connected

history.title: Collaboration History
history.description: View all operations performed in the session with timestamps and user info.
history.usage: Click Tools → Collaborate → View History. See a timeline of all changes.
tips: Click on any operation to revert to that point
History includes who made each change
Export history as JSON for documentation

undo_redo.title: Undo/Redo in Collaboration
undo_redo.description: Undo and redo work in collaborative sessions. Your changes don't affect others unless they conflict.
undo_redo.usage: Ctrl+Z to undo, Ctrl+Y to redo. Other users see your undo/redo as operations.
tips: Undoing remote operations may cause conflicts
Use chat to coordinate before undoing large changes

permissions.title: Collaboration Permissions
permissions.description: Control what team members can do in your session. Permissions include edit, view, comment.
permissions.usage: Host can set permissions per user. Right-click user in list → Change Permissions.
tips: Viewer: read-only access (no edits)
Commenter: can suggest changes via comments
Editor: full edit access

notifications.title: Collaboration Notifications
notifications.description: Get notified when users join/leave, when mentioned in chat, or on important changes.
notifications.usage: Notifications appear in bottom-right corner. Click to dismiss or act.
tips: Mute notifications in Settings → Notifications
Sound alerts only when tab is not focused

export_session.title: Export Collaboration Session
export_session.description: Save the entire session including geometry, history, and chat as a file.
export_session.usage: Tools → Collaborate → Export Session. Choose format (JSON, ZIP, PDF).
tips: JSON export includes full operation history for replay
ZIP includes geometry and chat log
PDF is human-readable documentation

webrtc.title: Peer-to-Peer (P2P) Data Transfer
webrtc.description: After initial signaling, direct P2P connections reduce latency. No data passes through server.
webrtc.usage: Automatic. Once WebRTC connects (within 5s), P2P data channel is established.
tips: P2P is faster and reduces server load
Falls back to WebSocket if P2P not available
Works through most firewalls via STUN/TURN

latency.title: Latency & Performance
latency.description: Monitor latency (ping time) to collaboration server. High latency may cause lag.
latency.usage: Status bar shows latency in ms. <50ms is excellent, <200ms is good.
tips: Latency is shown in parentheses: (47ms)
High latency affects cursor sharing more than geometry ops
Switch servers geographically closer if consistently high

troubleshooting.title: Collaboration Troubleshooting
troubleshooting.description: Common issues and solutions for collaboration features.
troubleshooting.usage: See detailed troubleshooting guide in Help → Troubleshooting → Collaboration.
tips: Check internet connection first
Clear browser cache if issues persist
Restart app if stuck in reconnecting state

bandwidth_limits.title: Bandwidth & Data Usage
bandwidth_limits.description: Collaboration uses minimal bandwidth. Typical usage is 1-5 MB per hour.
bandwidth_limits.usage: Cursor updates: 10 updates/sec × 100 bytes = 1 KB/sec max
tips: Operations are binary-encoded for efficiency
Chat is text-only, minimal bandwidth
Video/file sharing uses separate channels

performance.title: Performance in Collaboration
performance.description: Collaboration is optimized for performance. Even with 10 users, FPS remains high.
performance.usage: Monitor FPS counter (Ctrl+Shift+F). Should stay >30 FPS even with multiple collaborators.
tips: Turn off cursor sharing if FPS drops below 30
Use view filters to reduce geometry on screen
Close unused panels to save GPU memory

data_privacy.title: Data Privacy in Collaboration
data_privacy.description: All data is encrypted in transit (wss://) and at rest on server.
data_privacy.usage: Use HTTPS URL for secure transport. Server uses TLS 1.2+.
tips: Use password-protected rooms for sensitive projects
Don't share room ID publicly
Data is deleted 30 days after session ends

data_encryption.title: Data Encryption
data_encryption.description: All collaboration data is encrypted in transit and at rest.
data_encryption.usage: Automatic. Uses AES-256 for at-rest, TLS 1.2+ for in-transit.
tips: End-to-end encryption available for premium users
Server does not store encryption keys
